goal: Add a criterion-based throughput benchmark for CodeRankEmbed::embed and a parser/asserter shell script that prints `cpu_emb_per_sec=<N>` and exits 0 only when N >= 50 on CPU, per master-plan §4.2 line 303 and §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1789.
plan_summary: Wire `criterion = 0.5` into the workspace, add a `[[bench]]` section + dev-dep to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml`, create `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/throughput.rs` (a single criterion bench function `embed_100_snippets` that loads `CodeRankEmbed` from `ml/models/coderankembed/` once, iterates over 100 baked-in code snippets per iteration with `Throughput::Elements(100)`), create `scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh` (idempotent installer pre-flight, runs `cargo bench --bench throughput`, reads `target/criterion/embed_100_snippets/new/estimates.json`, computes `cpu_emb_per_sec = 1e9 / mean_ns_per_element`, prints `cpu_emb_per_sec=<N>` to stdout, exits 0 iff N >= 50 AND wall-time floor satisfied), and create `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh` (frozen-bench-script wrapper for verifier). NO new source modules in `src/`; NO ucil.toml plumbing; NO LanceDB writes; NO model download into the repo.
scope_in: Add to repo-root `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion just after `clap` or `clap_complete` — match existing alpha ordering): `criterion = { version = "0.5", default-features = false, features = ["cargo_bench_support", "plotters"] }`. The `cargo_bench_support` feature is required for `cargo bench` integration; `plotters` is the default plot backend. `default-features = false` keeps the dep tree minimal (drops the `rayon` feature which adds tokio-incompatible thread-pool churn). If `criterion` is already present in workspace deps, reuse the existing pin; otherwise add it.

Add to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion — after the existing `tempfile.workspace = true` line): `criterion.workspace = true`. Existing entries MUST keep their alphabetical position. AC02 verifies via `grep -E '^criterion\.workspace = true' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml`.

Add to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` a `[[bench]]` table at file END (after `[dev-dependencies]`): `[[bench]]` then `name = "throughput"` then `harness = false`. The `harness = false` is critical — it tells cargo to use criterion's main(), not Rust's default `#[test]` harness. AC03 verifies via `grep -nE '^\[\[bench\]\]' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` returns at least 1 hit.

Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/throughput.rs` (~120-180 LOC including the static `SNIPPETS` array). Module-level rustdoc preamble (`//!`) cites master-plan §4.2 line 303 ("50-150 embeddings/sec, ~137MB Int8") and §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1789 ("Benchmark: embedding throughput, query latency, recall@10") VERBATIM, names the feature ID `P2-W8-F06` and work-order `WO-0061`, and explains the bench shape (real CodeRankEmbed model load from `ml/models/coderankembed/`, 100 baked-in code snippets covering Rust + Python + TypeScript + plain text, `Throughput::Elements(100)` so criterion reports per-snippet throughput in elements/second).

In `benches/throughput.rs`, declare a `pub const SNIPPETS: [&str; 100] = [..]` literal array at module root containing 100 representative code snippets (a mix: 30 Rust functions / structs / impls; 25 Python defs / classes / decorators; 25 TypeScript functions / interfaces / classes; 20 plain-text doc-comment-style blocks). Each snippet between 50 and 400 bytes. The 100-element count is load-bearing — `Throughput::Elements(100)` matches the array length and the `mean_ns_per_element = mean_ns / 100` computation in the parser script.

In `benches/throughput.rs`, define `fn locate_model_dir() -> std::path::PathBuf` that walks up from `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` to find `ml/models/coderankembed/` — same upward-walk shape as `models.rs:test_coderankembed_inference` (line 441-463). On absence, panic with the operator-readable message: `panic!("ml/models/coderankembed/ not found — run scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh first")`. The bench is NOT skipped when the model is absent (unlike F02's `#[test]`); benches are gated by the verify script's pre-flight `install-coderankembed.sh` invocation.

In `benches/throughput.rs`, define the criterion bench function `fn bench_embed_100_snippets(c: &mut Criterion)`. Inside: load the model ONCE outside the bench loop (`let mut model = ucil_embeddings::models::CodeRankEmbed::load(&locate_model_dir()).expect("CodeRankEmbed::load")`); construct a `BenchmarkGroup` named `embed_100_snippets`; set `group.throughput(criterion::Throughput::Elements(100))`; set `group.sample_size(10)` (the default 100 is too slow for a 100-element-per-iter inner loop); call `group.bench_function("embed_100_snippets", |b| b.iter(|| { for s in SNIPPETS.iter() { criterion::black_box(model.embed(criterion::black_box(s)).expect("embed")); } }))`. Close the group via `group.finish()`. The `black_box` calls prevent the optimiser from caching the model output (they're only used for compile-time-known inputs, so the model.embed call won't be optimised away anyway, but explicit is better).

In `benches/throughput.rs`, declare the criterion entry-point at file END: `criterion::criterion_group!(benches, bench_embed_100_snippets);` and `criterion::criterion_main!(benches);`. AC09 verifies the macro names match.

Create NEW file `scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh` (~80-120 LOC) with `set -euo pipefail` at top. The script: (1) `cd` to repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. (2) Pre-flight `bash scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` (idempotent — fast path when already installed). (3) Run `cargo build --release -p ucil-embeddings --bench throughput` to warm the build cache (the first criterion sample on a debug build can dominate the measurement). (4) Run `cargo bench -p ucil-embeddings --bench throughput -- --noplot --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 10 embed_100_snippets`. The verbose flags are required because the bench's inner loop is 100 model.embed calls (each ~5-15ms on CPU) — default measurement-time of 5 seconds yields 50-100 iterations of the outer loop, which is a stable sample. (5) Read `target/criterion/embed_100_snippets/embed_100_snippets/new/estimates.json` (criterion's nested directory shape: `<group_id>/<bench_id>/new/estimates.json`). (6) Use jq to extract `.mean.point_estimate` (in nanoseconds; this is the per-iteration mean — i.e. ns per 100 snippets). (7) Compute `cpu_emb_per_sec` = $(echo "scale=2; 100 / ($MEAN_NS / 1e9)" | bc) — i.e. `100 / mean_seconds_per_iter`. Equivalent: `1e11 / $MEAN_NS`. (8) Print `cpu_emb_per_sec=<N>` to stdout (one line, exact prefix). (9) Wall-time floor: assert `MEAN_NS >= 1_000_000_000` (1 second per iteration of 100 snippets — i.e. ≤100/sec is the upper sanity bound; below 1s/iter the bench is implausibly fast and likely mock-shaped). Fail with stderr message if floor breached. (10) Throughput floor: assert `(echo "$cpu_emb_per_sec >= 50" | bc -l)` returns 1. Exit 0 on pass; exit non-zero with operator-readable stderr on any failure.

Create NEW file `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh` (~30-50 LOC) with `set -euo pipefail`. The script: (1) `cd` to repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. (2) Frozen-script existence check: `test -f scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh && test -x scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh`. (3) Frozen-bench-name selector check: `grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*group\.bench_function\("embed_100_snippets"' crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/throughput.rs` returns ≥1 hit. (4) Run `bash scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh` and capture its stdout. (5) Assert the captured stdout contains a line matching `^cpu_emb_per_sec=[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$` AND the parsed numeric value is `>= 50`. (6) Print `[OK] P2-W8-F06 cpu_emb_per_sec=<N>` on success; exit 0. Print operator-readable failure on stderr otherwise; exit 1.

Add lib.rs re-export discipline carry-forward: NONE this WO. The bench introduces ZERO new public symbols on `ucil-embeddings`'s `lib.rs` surface — all new code lives in `benches/`, which is a separate compilation unit. The 14-WO `lib.rs` re-export streak (WO-0042 → WO-0058 → WO-0059 → WO-0060) is unchanged because the streak measures NEW PUB SYMBOLS in `src/`, of which this WO has zero. AC explicitly verifies `git diff main...HEAD -- crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs` returns empty.

Pre-bake mutation #1 (CodeRankEmbed::embed body neutered): replace the `embed()` body's first executable statement with `let _ = (text, &mut self.session, &mut self.tokenizer); return Ok(vec![0.0_f32; ucil_embeddings::models::EMBEDDING_DIM]);`. This makes the function return a fixed dummy vector without running ONNX inference. Add `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return, clippy::let_unit_value)]` on the function for the mutation step. Expected runtime failure on `bash scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh`: the wall-time floor (≥1 second per iteration of 100 snippets) FAILS — a no-op return is sub-microsecond per call, so 100 calls is sub-millisecond, and `MEAN_NS ≪ 1_000_000_000`. The script's stderr emits: `[FAIL] wall-time floor breached: MEAN_NS=<small> < 1e9`. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs`. Rerun: green.

Pre-bake mutation #2 (SNIPPETS array shrunk): replace the SNIPPETS literal array with a 1-element array `["fn x() {}"]` (single short snippet). Expected runtime failure on `bash scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh`: the bench compiles AND runs, but `Throughput::Elements(100)` reports 100x-too-high throughput because the inner loop only runs 1 model.embed per iter, not 100. The script's `(100 / mean_seconds_per_iter)` math becomes `(100 / single_call_seconds)` which is artificially inflated. The wall-time floor (≥1s) ALSO fires because 1 embed call on CPU is ~5-15ms per call, NOT ≥1000ms. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/throughput.rs`. Rerun: green. (Both mutations corner the same axiom: the bench measures REAL ONNX work over EXACTLY 100 inputs.)

Pre-bake mutation #3 (script asserter neutered): replace the bench script's `cpu_emb_per_sec >= 50` comparison with `cpu_emb_per_sec >= 0` (always-pass). Expected runtime failure on `bash scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh`: the verify script does NOT delegate the threshold check to the bench script — it RE-CHECKS `>= 50` independently. So the verify script's stderr emits: `[FAIL] P2-W8-F06 cpu_emb_per_sec=<N> < 50` if the bench script's neutered check let through a sub-50/sec result. Restore: `git checkout -- scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh`. Rerun: green. (This mutation establishes that the verify script is the AUTHORITATIVE asserter, not the bench script — defence in depth.)

Commit cadence (~50 LOC soft target per root CLAUDE.md): EXPECTED 5 commits — (1) `build` (Cargo.toml workspace dep + Cargo.lock auto-sync), (2) `build` (crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml dev-dep + [[bench]] section), (3) `feat` (NEW benches/throughput.rs), (4) `feat` (NEW scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh + chmod), (5) `feat` (NEW scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh + chmod) + ready-for-review marker. The `Cargo.lock` auto-churn from `criterion`'s transitive deps (~15-25 added entries) is non-blocking per WO-0058 lessons line 569. The 100-element SNIPPETS array commit may push commit (3) to ~150-180 LOC; cite DEC-0005 module-coherence + WO-0048 297-LOC + WO-0056 542-LOC + WO-0058 305-LOC + WO-0059 425-LOC + WO-0060 503-LOC precedents inline. Single commit > 200 LOC is ALSO acceptable per the cumulative bundling-precedent — split is a soft preference, NOT a hard cap.

Commit-subject pre-flight (per WO-0059 lessons line 607 + WO-0060 lessons line 645): every commit subject ≤70 chars. Validated examples: `build(workspace): add criterion 0.5 dev-dep for embeddings bench` (62), `build(embeddings): add criterion dev-dep + [[bench]] throughput` (60), `feat(embeddings): add throughput criterion bench (P2-W8-F06)` (56), `feat(scripts): add bench-embed-throughput.sh asserter` (51), `feat(scripts): add verify/P2-W8-F06.sh frozen wrapper` (52). Pre-flight check via `git log --oneline | head -5 | awk -F' ' '{ print length($0) }'` returning all values ≤80 (subject + 8-char sha + 1 space).
scope_out: NO new source modules in `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/` — this WO ONLY adds `benches/throughput.rs` and shell scripts. The frozen public API surface of `ucil-embeddings` (CodeRankEmbed + EmbeddingChunker + OnnxSession types) is UNCHANGED.

NO `ucil.toml` config plumbing for the bench — the model location is hard-coded as `ml/models/coderankembed/` per F02 precedent (`models.rs:441-463`). Future work-orders may introduce config-driven model paths (P2-W8-F03 Qwen3 GPU upgrade is the natural site).
NO LanceDB writes — F06 is throughput-only. F04 (LanceDB background indexing) is the natural consumer of the throughput characterisation.

NO model download into the repo — the `ml/models/coderankembed/` artefacts are operator-installed via `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` (created in WO-0059). The bench ASSUMES the artefacts are present; the bench script's pre-flight runs the installer (idempotent fast-path).
NO recall@10 benchmark — that's the third W8 benchmark per master-plan §1789. Recall@10 needs a labelled query/answer fixture set; out of scope for F06 throughput-only.
NO query latency benchmark — that's P2-W8-F07 (separate feature; depends on F04 for indexed data).
NO Qwen3-Embedding GPU bench path — F03 (Qwen3 config gate) is the natural site; this WO does not touch GPU EP wiring.

NO `tracing` instrumentation on `embed()` — the criterion bench measures the function in isolation; tracing spans introduce 100-500ns overhead per span which is signal-noise for a 5-15ms inference call. Future WOs may add `tracing` instrumentation for production observability; NOT in scope here.
NO `executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` (P2-W8-F04 selector) — F04 is in a separate WO; this WO does not touch `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`.
NO touch to `feat/WO-0053-lancedb-per-branch` orphan-branch state — DEC-0016 documents the gap; F06 does not depend on `BranchManager` so the gap does not block this WO.
NO `ndarray 0.16 vs 0.17` workspace alignment — carry from WO-0058 lessons line 555. F06 does not touch ndarray; gap is deferred again.

NO production wiring of 4 G1Source impls (TreeSitter / Serena / AstGrep / Diagnostics) into `find_references` — still deferred to P2-W7-F05 per WO-0047/0048/0050/0051/0055/0056/0058/0059/0060 standing scope_out. WO-0061 adds zero G1 wiring.
NO `tracing` instrumentation on `execute_g1` / `fuse_g1` — still deferred to P2-W7-F05 per WO-0047 lessons. WO-0061 does not touch this code path.

AC36 — Pre-baked mutation #3 (script asserter neutered) per scope_in[16]: editing `scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh` to relax `cpu_emb_per_sec >= 50` to `cpu_emb_per_sec >= 0` (always-pass) MUST NOT regress `bash scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh` — because the verify script INDEPENDENTLY re-checks `>= 50`. The verify script's stderr should emit `[FAIL] P2-W8-F06 cpu_emb_per_sec=<N> < 50` only if the actual `<N>` is below 50; if `<N>` is naturally above 50, both bench script (always-pass) and verify script (≥50) emit success. The mutation establishes that the verify script is the AUTHORITATIVE asserter — its `>= 50` check MUST be present and tested. Restore: `git checkout -- scripts/bench-embed-throughput.sh`. Rerun: green.

AC37 — Commit cadence: at least 5 commits on the feature branch per scope_in[17]. `git rev-list --count main..HEAD` returns ≥5. Per root CLAUDE.md commit cadence ('one logical change per commit, max ~50 LOC of diff per commit'). DEC-0005 module-coherence permits up to ~200 LOC for a coherent test+helper commit (see WO-0046 252-LOC, WO-0048 297-LOC, WO-0056 542-LOC, WO-0058 305-LOC, WO-0059 425-LOC, WO-0060 503-LOC precedents). Commit (3) (the bench file with 100-element SNIPPETS array) may push to ~150-200 LOC; cite the precedent stack inline in the commit body.
AC38 — Commit-subject ≤70 chars: `git log --format='%s' main..HEAD` shows every subject ≤70 characters. Per WO-0059 lessons line 607 + WO-0060 lessons line 645 planner-emit checklist.

AC39 — Branch is up-to-date with `origin`: `git rev-parse HEAD` matches `git rev-parse @{u}`. No uncommitted changes (`git status --porcelain` empty). The ready-for-review marker `ucil-build/work-orders/0061-ready-for-review.md` is committed and pushed.

AC40 — Frozen-bench-script wall-time pre-flight: the verifier MUST budget 90-180 seconds wall-time for the criterion bench run (5-second `--measurement-time` × ~10-25 outer iterations × 1.5x warm-up factor). On first-run after `cargo clean`, ALSO budget the cargo build cache miss (60-120 seconds for `criterion` + transitive crate compile). Total verifier wall-time budget: ≤5 minutes. If the bench wall-time exceeds 5 minutes, raise an escalation; do NOT relax the wall-time floor (per root CLAUDE.md `Test suite wall-time >2× 7-day trailing median` escalation trigger).
